name: Emberwick
concept: A shy, dumpling-shaped ash moth who carries the very last coal of an old fire behind a little stove-door in its belly, and hugs itself all night so the ember never goes out.
imagePrompt:
Chunky pixel-art creature sprite, full body, standing, facing viewer, symmetrical, drawn on a coarse pixel grid (roughly 64x64 logical pixels blown up large, every pixel a crisp hard-edged square, no anti-aliasing, no gradients — shading done only with hard colour steps and bold checkerboard dithering). Strict limited palette of about twelve colours.

THE CREATURE: a plump, dumpling-round moth the size and weight of a bread loaf. Silhouette is one solid connected teardrop: a big soft egg-shaped body, wider at the bottom, with its heavy wings folded down and closed around it like a fastened wool cloak, forming a rounded bell-shaped skirt that rests flat on the ground. The whole outer edge is a clean, definite, chunky staircase outline in dark charcoal-brown (2 pixels thick), fully closed, no gaps, no wisps, no floating bits, no sparks, nothing detached.

WINGS: warm ash-grey to soft mushroom-taupe, matte and powdery like fireplace ash, patterned with wide banded dithering and two bold rounded "eyespot" markings low on the cloak — each a thick ring of dusty rose around a deep charcoal centre. Wing edges are scalloped in big chunky 4-pixel-wide scallops, like the cut edge of thick felt. No fringe, no fur, no fuzz, no thin hairs anywhere.

THE ONE IDEA — THE BELLY STOVE: set into the middle of its chest is a small rounded stove-door: a chunky iron-dark frame with three fat horizontal grate bars, and behind it a single glowing coal — a warm ember gradient stepping from deep maroon through orange to a pale butter-yellow core. The glow spills outward in hard-edged concentric bands of warm light across the surrounding ash-grey chest and up under its chin, so the belly is the brightest thing in the sprite and lights the creature from within. The stove-door is part of the body, moulded into it, not an attached object.

POSE: both stubby rounded arms are wrapped around its own belly, hugging the stove-door, fat mitten paws overlapping in front, keeping the coal warm. Two short stumpy feet peek out at the bottom of the cloak, warm dark grey, each 6+ pixels wide.

FACE: sits high on the body, big and honest. Two large oval eyes, each a solid ember: dark maroon rim, bright orange iris, and one square white pixel highlight in the upper left of each. A tiny soft blush of dusty rose on each cheek. A very small, calm, closed smile — just a few pixels — content and a little sleepy. Utterly lovable, wants to be looked after.

ANTENNAE: two short, thick, sturdy antennae curling gently up and inward like fat comma shapes, made of stacked chunky pixel blocks at least 4 pixels thick along their entire length, tapering to a rounded club tip. Solid, never thin, never wispy, fully attached to the head.

PALETTE: warm ash grey, mushroom taupe, charcoal-brown, dusty rose, deep maroon, ember orange, butter-yellow, one cream highlight. Cosy, banked-fire warmth against cold ash. Bold readable shapes at tiny size, nothing finer than four pixels, no text, no symbols, no particles.
